### Session Management
[...]
1. A server using the Streamable HTTP transport **MAY** assign a session ID at
 initialization time, by including it in an `Mcp-Session-Id` header on the HTTP
 response containing the `InitializeResult`.
 - The session ID **SHOULD** be globally unique and cryptographically secure (e.g., a
 securely generated UUID, a JWT, or a cryptographic hash).
 - The session ID **MUST** only contain visible ASCII characters (ranging from 0x21 to
 0x7E).
[...]
2. If an `Mcp-Session-Id` is returned by the server during initialization, clients using
 the Streamable HTTP transport **MUST** include it in the `Mcp-Session-Id` header on
 all of their subsequent HTTP requests.
 - Servers that require a session ID **SHOULD** respond to requests without an
 `Mcp-Session-Id` header (other than initialization) with HTTP 400 Bad Request.
[...]
### Backwards Compatibility
[...]
Clients and servers can maintain backwards compatibility with the deprecated HTTP+SSE
transport (from
protocol version 2024-11-05) as follows:
[...]
**Servers** wanting to support older clients should:
[...]
- Continue to host both the SSE and POST endpoints of the old transport, alongside the
 new "MCP endpoint" defined for the Streamable HTTP transport.
 - It is also possible to combine the old POST endpoint and the new MCP endpoint, but
 this may introduce unneeded complexity.
[...]
**Clients** wanting to support older servers should:
[...]
1. Accept an MCP server URL from the user, which may point to either a server using the
 old transport or the new transport.
[...]
2. Attempt to POST an `InitializeRequest` to the server URL, with an `Accept` header as
 defined above:
 - If it succeeds, the client can assume this is a server supporting the new Streamable
 HTTP transport.
 - If it fails with an HTTP 4xx status code (e.g., 405 Method Not Allowed or 404 Not
 Found):
 - Issue a GET request to the server URL, expecting that this will open an SSE stream
 and return an `endpoint` event as the first event.

 the old HTTP+SSE transport, and should use that transport for all subsequent

Title: HTTP Streaming
URL: https://microsoft-mcp-for-beginners.mintlify.app/modules/03-http-streaming
Published: N/A
Author: N/A
Highlights:
> Use Streamable HTTP — the recommended transport for remote MCP servers — to send real-time progress notifications to clients.
[...]
Streamable HTTP is the recommended transport for remote MCP servers (MCP Specification 2025-11-25). It enables real-time progress notifications via a structured JSON-RPC notification system, making it ideal for cloud deployments, multi-client scenarios, and long-running AI tasks.
[...]
| Transport | Real-time updates | Streaming | Scalability | Recommended for |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| stdio | No | No | Low | Local CLI tools |
| SSE (deprecated) | Yes | Yes | Medium | — |
| Streamable HTTP | Yes | Yes | High | Cloud, multi-client |
[...]
MCP streaming is not about chunked HTTP responses. Instead it uses a structured notification system:
[...]
| Feature | Classic HTTP streaming | MCP streaming (notifications) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main response | Chunked | Single, at end |
| Progress updates | Sent as data chunks | Sent as separate notifications |
| Client requirements | Process stream | Implement message handler |
| Message format | Plain text chunks | Structured JSON-RPC objects |
[...]
A notification is a JSON-RPC message the server sends to the client to report progress during a long-running operation:
[...]
```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "notifications/message",
  "params": {
    "level": "info",
    "data": "Processing document 3/10"
  }
}
[...]
# Use streamable-http transport
mcp.run(transport="streamable-http")
[...]
1. Update server code to use `transport="streamable-http"` in `mcp.run()`.
2. Update client code to use `streamablehttp_client` instead of the SSE client.
3. Implement a `message_handler` in the client to process notifications.
4. Test with existing tools and workflows.
5. Optionally run both SSE and Streamable HTTP in parallel on different endpoints during the transition.
[...]
- Streamable HTTP is the recommended transport for remote MCP servers (MCP spec 2025-11-25).
- MCP streaming uses a notification system — the final result is still a single response, but progress is sent as separate messages.
- Always validate the `Origin` header and use HTTPS in production deployments.
- Migrating from the deprecated SSE transport to Streamable HTTP requires only minor changes to both server and client.

---

Title: SEP-2243: HTTP Header Standardization for Streamable HTTP Transport - Model Context Protocol
URL: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/seps/2243-http-standardization
Published: N/A
Author: N/A
Highlights:
# SEP-2243: HTTP Header Standardization for Streamable HTTP Transport
[...]
This SEP proposes exposing critical routing and context information in standard HTTP header locations for the Streamable HTTP transport. By mirroring key fields from the JSON-RPC payload into HTTP headers, network intermediaries such as load balancers, proxies, and observability tools can route and process MCP traffic without deep packet inspection, reducing latency and computational overhead.
[...]
Current MCP implementations over HTTP bury all routing information within the JSON-RPC payload. This creates friction for network infrastructure:
[...]
The Streamable HTTP transport will require POST requests to include the following headers mirrored from the request body:
[...]
| Header Name | Source Field | Required For |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Mcp-Method` | `method` | All requests and notifications |
| `Mcp-Name` | `params.name` or `params.uri` | `tools/call`, `resources/read`, `prompts/get` requests |
[...]
These headers are required for compliance with the MCP version in which they are introduced.
[...]
Server Behavior: Servers that process the request body MUST reject requests where the values specified in the headers do not match the values in the request body.
[...]
### Custom Headers from Tool Parameters
[...]
MCP servers MAY designate specific tool parameters to be mirrored into HTTP headers using an `x-mcp-header` extension property in the parameter's schema within the tool's `inputSchema`.
[...]
Client Requirement: While the use of `x-mcp-header` is optional for servers, clients MUST support this feature. When a server's tool definition includes `x-mcp-header` annotations, conforming clients MUST mirror the designated parameter values into HTTP headers as specified in this document.
[...]
The `x-mcp-header` property specifies the name portion used to construct the header name `Mcp-Param-{name}`.
[...]
- MUST NOT be empty
- MUST match HTTP field-name token syntax (`1*tchar`, RFC 9110 Section 5.1)
- MUST NOT contain control characters, including carriage return (CR, `\r`) or line feed (LF, `\n`)
- MUST be case-insensitively unique among all `x-mcp-header` values in the `inputSchema`
- MUST only be applied to parameters with primitive types (integer, string, boolean). Parameters with type `number` are not permitted. Integer values MUST be within the safe range for JavaScript (−2^53+1 to 2^53−1)
- MAY be applied to properties at any nesting depth within the `inputSchema`, not only top-level properties
[...]
Clients using the Streamable HTTP transport MUST reject tool definitions where any `x-mcp-header` value violates these constraints. Rejection means the client MUST exclude the invalid tool from the result of `tools/list`. Clients SHOULD log a warning when rejecting a tool definition, including the tool name and the reason for rejection. This behavior ensures that a single malformed tool definition does not prevent other valid tools from being used. Clients using other transports (e.g., stdio) MAY ignore `x-mcp-header` annotations entirely.
[...]
With This Proposal: The client detects the `x-mcp-header` annotation and automatically adds the header `Mcp-Param-Region: us-west1` to the HTTP request. The load balancer can now route based on the header without parsing the body.
[...]
### Header Processing
[...]
Clients MUST encode parameter values before including them in HTTP headers to ensure safe transmission and prevent injection attacks.
[...]
HTTP parsers typically trim leading and trailing whitespace from header values. To preserve leading and trailing spaces in parameter values, clients MUST use Base64 encoding when the value:
[...]
When a value cannot be safely represented as a plain ASCII header value, clients MUST use Base64 encoding of the UTF-8 representation of the value with the following format:
[...]
#### Client Behavior
[...]
When constructing a `tools/call` request via HTTP transport, the client MUST:
[...]
1. Extract the values for any standard headers from the request body (e.g., `method`, `params.name`, `params.uri`)
2. Append the `Mcp-Method` header and, if applicable, `Mcp-Name` header to the request
3. Inspect the tool's `inputSchema` for properties marked with `x-mcp-header` and extract the value for each parameter
4. Encode the values according to the rules in Value Encoding
5. Append a `Mcp-Param-{Name}: {Value}` header to the request:
[...]

parts.text:
Great, now I have a comprehensive understanding of MCP Streamable HTTP transport and the Go library support.

Key findings:

1. **`github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go`** (v0.54.1 latest) fully supports Streamable HTTP transport:
   - `server.NewStreamableHTTPServer(mcpServer, opts...)` - server-side implementation
   - `server.WithEndpointPath("/mcp")` - configurable endpoint
   - `server.WithStateful(true)` - stateful sessions
   - TLS support via `server.WithTLSCert(certFile, keyFile)`
   - It's a `http.Handler`, easy to integrate with our monitoring server

2. **Streamable HTTP Protocol** (MCP spec 2025-03-26+):
   - Single HTTP endpoint, both POST and GET
   - POST: send JSON-RPC messages (requests/notifications/responses)
   - GET: optional long-lived SSE stream for server-to-client messages
   - Session management via `Mcp-Session-Id` header
   - Response can be `application/json` (single) or `text/event-stream` (with SSE)
   - Replace old HTTP+SSE transport from 2024-11-05

3. There's also an official Go SDK: `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` (v1.6.1), which also supports Streamable HTTP

For our project, `mark3labs/mcp-go` is the better choice because:
- More mature and widely used (8756 stars, 210 contributors)
- Simpler API, good documentation
- Active development (latest release v0.54.1 on 2026-05-25)
- Has Streamable HTTP server built in

Now let me update the plan with this information.

The MCP integration will use `mark3labs/mcp-go` with `StreamableHTTPServer` transport. The MCP tools will allow AI assistants to:
1. Query target status and metrics
2. Add/remove/update monitoring targets
3. Trigger configuration reload

Let me present the updated architecture showing the Streamable HTTP transport layer.

确认了。**Streamable HTTP** 是 MCP 规范的推荐传输层（MCP 2025-03-26+），替代了旧的 HTTP+SSE。我们用 `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go`（v0.54.1，8765 stars）来实现，它内置完整的支持。

### Streamable HTTP 协议栈

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│            MCP 应用层                      │
│   Tools: get_targets / add_target / etc   │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│            JSON-RPC 2.0                   │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│        Streamable HTTP Transport          │
│                                           │
│   POST /mcp  ── 发送 request/notification │
│   GET  /mcp  ── 可选长连接 SSE 监听       │
│   DELETE      ── 终止 session             │
│                                           │
│   Header: Mcp-Session-Id (会话标识)       │
│   Header: Mcp-Protocol-Version (版本)     │
│   Header: Mcp-Method / Mcp-Name (路由)    │
│                                           │
│   响应: application/json (单条)           │
│         或 text/event-stream (SSE 流)     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

│  ┌─────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────┐       │
│  │  Metric Pipeline    │  │   MCP Streamable HTTP    │       │
│  │  Buffer → Batch     │  │   POST/GET /mcp          │       │
│  │  → VM Push          │  │                          │       │
│  └─────┬───────────────┘  │  Tools:                  │       │
│        │                  │   get_targets             │       │
│  ┌─────▼───────────────┐  │   get_metrics             │       │
│  │ VictoriaMetrics     │  │   add_target              │       │
│  │ /api/v1/import/     │  │   remove_target           │       │
│  │   prometheus        │  │   update_target           │       │
│  └─────────────────────┘  │   reload_config           │       │
│                           └──────────────────────────┘       │
│                                         :9801                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

### MCP 模块实现结构（更新）

```go
internal/mcp/
├── server.go          // StreamableHTTPServer 初始化 + 启动
├── tools.go           // Tool 定义（inputSchema）
├── tools_query.go     // 查询类工具实现
├── tools_manage.go    // 管理类工具实现
└── server_test.go     // 集成测试
```

核心代码模式：

```go
// internal/mcp/server.go
mcpServer := server.NewMCPServer("probe-agent", "1.0.0")

// 注册工具
mcpServer.AddTool(server.NewTool("get_targets", ...))
mcpServer.AddTool(server.NewTool("add_target", ...))
// ...

// Streamable HTTP 传输
httpServer := server.NewStreamableHTTPServer(mcpServer,
    server.WithEndpointPath("/mcp"),
)
httpServer.Start(":9801")
```

---

### 与 Claude Code 集成

在 `claude.json` 或 `settings.local.json` 中：

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "probe-agent": {
      "type": "url",
      "url": "http://localhost:9801/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

然后你可以直接对 Claude 说："看一下 8.8.8.8 的 ICMP 延迟"或"把 192.168.1.100 加到 SNMP 监控里，60s 采集一次"。

---

### 最终确认方案

| 项目 | 方案 |
|------|------|
| **语言** | Go |
| **项目名** | `probe-agent` |
| **ICMP** | `golang.org/x/net/icmp`，多包突发 + 直方图/jitter/stddev |
| **SNMP** | `github.com/gosnmp/gosnmp`，per-target 独立 goroutine + 独立超时 |
| **DNS** | Go `net.Lookup*`，支持多记录类型 + 指定 DNS 服务器 |
| **输出** | VictoriaMetrics `/api/v1/import/prometheus`，批处理 + 环形缓冲区 |
| **配置** | YAML，按模块拆分：`config/{global,icmp,snmp,dns}.yaml` |
| **MCP** | `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go`，**Streamable HTTP** 传输，`:9801/mcp` |
| **MCP 工具** | 查询类(get_targets/get_metrics) + 管理类(add/remove/update_target/reload) |
| **运行方式** | root 直接运行二进制 |
| **热重载** | SIGHUP 触发配置重新加载 |

如果没问题，我开始实现 Phase 1（项目骨架 + 配置加载 + 日志 + VM 推送管道）。确认一下？

The user wants MCP authentication. Let me think about what authentication mechanisms are available for MCP Streamable HTTP.

Looking at the search results, I found that `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` supports:

1. **OAuth 2.0** - `server.WithProtectedResourceMetadata()` for OAuth-protected resources
2. **CORS** - `server.WithStreamableHTTPCORS()` for browser-based clients

But the user might want something simpler like:
- **Bearer Token (JWT)** - Custom authentication via middleware
- **API Key** - Simple token-based auth
- **mTLS** - Mutual TLS

Since `StreamableHTTPServer` is an `http.Handler`, we can wrap it with standard HTTP middleware for authentication. This gives us maximum flexibility.

Let me think about the best approach:

1. **HTTP Middleware Wrapping** - Since StreamableHTTPServer implements `http.Handler`, we can wrap it with auth middleware. This is the most flexible approach.

2. **Built-in OAuth** - The library supports OAuth 2.0 via `WithProtectedResourceMetadata`.

3. **Custom `WithHTTPContextFunc`** - The library allows extracting context from HTTP requests, which could be used for auth validation.

For a monitoring tool, the most practical approaches would be:
- **API Key / Bearer Token** (simple, common for monitoring tools)
- **JWT** (for more complex setups)
- **OAuth 2.0** (for enterprise/gateway scenarios)

I should present options to the user and let them choose. Let me ask about what auth mechanism they prefer.

Actually, looking at the MCP spec and the library more carefully:

The `StreamableHTTPServer` supports `WithHTTPContextFunc` which allows extracting custom context from HTTP requests. We could implement a custom context function that validates tokens.

Also, the `server.WithProtectedResourceMetadata()` provides OAuth support with authorization server integration.

For a practical monitoring tool, I think we should offer:

1. **Option A: API Key (simplest)** - Static key in config, validated via middleware
2. **Option B: JWT** - Configurable secret/public key, validated via middleware  
3. **Option C: OAuth 2.0** - Built-in `WithProtectedResourceMetadata` support

Let me present these options to the user.
明白。`mark3labs/mcp-go` 的 `StreamableHTTPServer` 本质是一个 `http.Handler`，可以通过标准 HTTP 中间件层加入鉴权。有几种方案：

parts.text:
The user chose API Key (recommended). Now let me design the authentication layer.

The implementation approach:

1. **HTTP Middleware** wrapping `StreamableHTTPServer`
2. Check `Authorization: Bearer <api-key>` header
3. Configurable in `global.yaml` under `mcp_server.auth`
4. Support multiple keys for rotation
5. Proper error responses (401 Unauthorized)

Let me design the config structure and the middleware approach.

For the config:

```yaml
mcp_server:
  enabled: true
  listen: ":9801"
  auth:
    type: api_key
    keys:
      - "sk-probe-agent-xxxxx"
      - "sk-probe-agent-yyyyy"  # 滚动更新时使用
```

The middleware would:
1. Extract `Authorization` header
2. Verify it's `Bearer <key>` format
3. Check key against configured list
4. Return `401 Unauthorized` with JSON-RPC error if invalid

Since `StreamableHTTPServer` is an `http.Handler`, we wrap it:

```go
func AuthMiddleware(next http.Handler, config *config.AuthConfig) http.Handler {
    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        auth := r.Header.Get("Authorization")
        if !strings.HasPrefix(auth, "Bearer ") {
            http.Error(w, `{"error":"missing or invalid authorization"}`, http.StatusUnauthorized)
            return
        }
        key := strings.TrimPrefix(auth, "Bearer ")
        if !config.IsValidKey(key) {
            http.Error(w, `{"error":"invalid api key"}`, http.StatusUnauthorized)
            return
        }
        next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
    })
}
```

This is simple, clean, and secure enough for an internal monitoring tool.

Let me update the design plan with this.

好，**API Key** 方案，设计如下：

### Config 层

**config/global.yaml**
```yaml
mcp_server:
  enabled: true
  listen: ":9801"
  auth:
    type: api_key
    keys:
      - "sk-probe-xxxxx"          # 主密钥
      - "sk-probe-yyyyy"          # 轮换时的备用密钥
```

### 实现层

```
内部/mcp/
├── server.go          // 初始化 MCPServer + StreamableHTTPServer
├── auth.go            // API Key Middleware
├── tools.go           // Tool 定义
├── tools_query.go     // 查询工具实现
└── tools_manage.go    // 管理工具实现
```

`StreamableHTTPServer` 是标准 `http.Handler`，用中间件包裹：

```
                            ┌───────────────┐
  POST /mcp  ──────────►    │  Auth         │
  Header:                    │  Middleware   │
  Authorization: Bearer sk  │              │
                            └──────┬────────┘
                                   │ 通过
                                   ▼
                            ┌───────────────┐
                            │ StreamableHTTP │
                            │ Server         │
                            │ POST/GET/DELETE│
                            └───────────────┘
```

```go
// internal/mcp/auth.go
func AuthMiddleware(next http.Handler, validKeys []string) http.Handler {
    keySet := make(map[string]struct{}, len(validKeys))
    for _, k := range validKeys {
        keySet[k] = struct{}{}
    }

    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        auth := r.Header.Get("Authorization")

        if !strings.HasPrefix(auth, "Bearer ") {
            writeUnauthorized(w, "missing authorization header")
            return
        }

        key := strings.TrimPrefix(auth, "Bearer ")
        if _, ok := keySet[key]; !ok {
            writeUnauthorized(w, "invalid api key")
            return
        }

        next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
    })
}

func writeUnauthorized(w http.ResponseWriter, msg string) {
    w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
    w.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)
    json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{
        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
        "error":   msg,
    })
}
```

### 客户端配置

Claude Code `claude.json` 接入：

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "probe-agent": {
      "type": "url",
      "url": "http://localhost:9801/mcp",
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer sk-probe-xxxxx"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### 要点

| 维度 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| **Header** | `Authorization: Bearer <key>` |
| **失败响应** | HTTP 401 + JSON-RPC error |
| **多密钥** | 支持多个 key 同时有效，方便密钥轮换 |
| **热加载** | 密钥变更通过 SIGHUP 热重载自动生效 |
| **日志** | 鉴权失败记录 warn 日志（含客户端 IP，不记录密钥） |

需要我把这个鉴权设计也合并到最终方案中确认吗？

## 最终方案确认：`probe-agent`

### 项目概览

Go 语言编写的网络监控采集器，替代 Categraf 的 ICMP/SNMP/DNS 插件，输出到 VictoriaMetrics，内置 MCP Streamable HTTP Server 供 AI 助手交互。

### 核心架构

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         probe-agent                              │
│                                                                  │
│  配置文件                                                         │
│  config/global.yaml (全局+VM+MCP)                                │
│  config/icmp.yaml   (ICMP目标)                                   │
│  config/snmp.yaml   (SNMP目标+OID)                               │
│  config/dns.yaml    (DNS目标)                                    │
│          │                                                        │
│          ▼ 热加载(SIGHUP)                                         │
│  ┌──────────────┐                                                │
│  │ Config Loader │                                               │
│  └──────┬───────┘                                                │
│         │                                                         │
│         ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│         │          ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐    │   │
│         │          │ICMP Mgr  │ │SNMP Mgr  │ │DNS Mgr   │    │   │
│         │          │ ┌───┐    │ │ ┌───┐    │ │ ┌───┐    │    │   │
│         │          │ │t1 │◄──►│ │ │t1 │◄──►│ │ │t1 │◄──►│    │   │
│         │          │ │t2 │    │ │ │t2 │    │ │ │t2 │    │    │   │
│         │          │ │.. │    │ │ │.. │    │ │ │.. │    │    │   │
│         │          │ └───┘    │ │ └───┘    │ │ └───┘    │    │   │
│         │          └────┬─────┘ └────┬─────┘ └────┬─────┘    │   │
│         │               └──────┬─────┴──────┬──────┘         │   │
│         │                      ▼            ▼                 │   │
│         │  ┌─────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  │   │
│         │  │   Metric Pipeline       │  │  MCP Server      │  │   │
│         │  │   Buffer → Batch        │  │  Streamable HTTP │  │   │
│         │  │   → VM Push             │  │  POST/GET /mcp   │  │   │
│         │  └──────────┬──────────────┘  └───────┬──────────┘  │   │
│         │             │                          │              │   │
│         │             ▼                          │ Auth MW     │   │
│         │  VictoriaMetrics            ┌──────────▼──────────┐  │   │
│         │  /api/v1/import/prometheus  │ Bearer <api-key>    │  │   │
│         └─────────────────────────────┘                    │  │   │

### 详细设计

#### 1. 配置拆分

| 文件 | 内容 |
|------|------|
| `config/global.yaml` | `concurrency`, `log_level`, `victoria_metrics`（push_url/batch/flush_interval/buffer/retry）, `mcp_server`（enabled/listen/auth.type/auth.keys） |
| `config/icmp.yaml` | `histogram_buckets_ms`, `targets[]`（host/interval/count/timeout/labels） |
| `config/snmp.yaml` | `defaults`（version/community/port/timeout/retries）, `targets[]`（host/interval/labels/oids.scalar/tables） |
| `config/dns.yaml` | `targets[]`（domain/server/record_type/interval） |

#### 2. ICMP 采集

- 依赖：`golang.org/x/net/icmp` + `ipv4`
- 每次探测发 `count` 个包，记录每个 RTT
- 输出指标：
  - `icmp_rtt_min / max / avg / stddev`
  - `icmp_jitter`（连续延迟差的均值）
  - `icmp_packet_loss_ratio`
  - `icmp_consecutive_loss`
  - `icmp_rtt_bucket{le="..."}`（可配直方图桶）
  - `icmp_probe_duration_seconds`
  - `icmp_up{target}`
- 每个目标独立 goroutine + ticker

#### 3. SNMP 采集

- 依赖：`github.com/gosnmp/gosnmp`
- **每目标独立连接 + 独立超时/重试**，互不阻塞
- 使用 `errgroup` + 信号量控制并发
- OID 类型：
  - 标量（Get）：如 `1.3.6.1.2.1.1.1.0` → `snmp_sys_descr`
  - 表（Walk/BulkWalk）：如 `1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.1` → `snmp_if_in_octets{ifIndex="1"}`
- 失败打标 `snmp_up{target}=0`，不影响其他目标

#### 4. DNS 采集

- 使用 `net.Resolver` + 自定义 `Dial` 指定 DNS 服务器
- 记录类型：A / AAAA / MX / NS / CNAME / TXT
- 输出指标：
  - `dns_lookup_duration_seconds{domain,server,record_type}`
  - `dns_up{domain,server}`
  - `dns_response_code`（NOERROR / NXDOMAIN / SERVFAIL）
  - `dns_answer_count`

#### 5. Metric Pipeline → VictoriaMetrics

```
采集器 goroutine ──channel──► Metric Batcher ──► HTTP Pusher ──► VM
                              (按批/定时刷新)    (环形缓冲+重试)
```

- 格式：Prometheus 文本格式推送至 `/api/v1/import/prometheus`
- 环形缓冲区大小可配，防止 VM 短时不可用丢数据
- 自动重试 + 指数退避

#### 6. MCP Server（Streamable HTTP）

- 依赖：`github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go`
- 传输：**Streamable HTTP**（单端点 `/mcp`，POST/GET/DELETE）
- 端口：`:9801`
- 实现方式：`server.NewStreamableHTTPServer()` + 标准 `http.Handler` 接口
- 可以嵌入已有 HTTP server 或独立运行

**工具列表：**

| 工具 | 类型 | 功能 |
|------|------|------|
| `get_targets(module, status)` | 查询 | 查看目标列表 |
| `get_metrics(target, module)` | 查询 | 查询最新指标 |
| `get_icmp_stats(target)` | 查询 | ICMP 延迟/抖动/丢包详情 |
| `get_snmp_data(target, oid)` | 查询 | SNMP 采集数据 |
| `add_target(module, config)` | 管理 | 新增监控目标 |
| `remove_target(module, host)` | 管理 | 移除监控目标 |
| `update_target(module, host, config)` | 管理 | 修改目标配置 |
| `reload_config()` | 管理 | 触发配置热重载 |

**鉴权（API Key）：**

```yaml
# config/global.yaml
mcp_server:
  enabled: true
  listen: ":9801"
  auth:
    type: api_key
    keys:
      - "sk-probe-xxxxx"
```

```
请求头: Authorization: Bearer sk-probe-xxxxx
失败:   HTTP 401 + {"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":"invalid api key"}
```

中间件实现：`http.Handler` 包装，仅在 POST 前校验 `Authorization` 头。支持多密钥轮换。

#### 7. 项目结构

```
probe-agent/
├── cmd/probe-agent/main.go
├── internal/
│   ├── config/
│   │   ├── config.go           # 配置加载 + 热重载
│   │   ├── global.go
│   │   ├── icmp.go
│   │   ├── snmp.go
│   │   └── dns.go
│   ├── collector/
│   │   ├── manager.go          # 生命周期管理
│   │   ├── icmp.go
│   │   ├── snmp.go
│   │   └── dns.go
│   ├── metrics/
│   │   ├── metric.go
│   │   ├── histogram.go
│   │   └── registry.go
│   ├── output/
│   │   ├── output.go           # Output 接口
│   │   ├── vmpush.go           # VM 推送
│   │   ├── buffer.go           # 环形缓冲
│   │   └── batch.go            # 批处理
│   └── mcp/
│       ├── server.go           # MCPServer + StreamableHTTP 初始化
│       ├── auth.go             # API Key 中间件
│       ├── tools.go            # 工具注册
│       ├── tools_query.go      # 查询实现
│       └── tools_manage.go     # 管理实现
├── config/
│   ├── global.yaml
│   ├── icmp.yaml
│   ├── snmp.yaml
│   └── dns.yaml
├── go.mod
└── go.sum
```

#### 8. 关键依赖

| 依赖 | 版本 | 用途 |
|------|------|------|
| `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` | v0.54.1 | MCP Server + Streamable HTTP |
| `golang.org/x/net` | latest | ICMP raw socket |
| `github.com/gosnmp/gosnmp` | latest | SNMP Get/Walk |
| `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` | latest | 配置解析 |
| `go.uber.org/zap` | latest | 结构化日志 |

#### 9. 实施阶段

| 阶段 | 内容 | 依赖 |
|------|------|------|
| **Phase 1** | 项目骨架：main 入口、拆分配置加载、zap 日志、热重载信号 | 无 |
| **Phase 2** | Metric Pipeline：Metric 结构体、环形缓冲、批处理、VM HTTP 推送 | Phase 1 |
| **Phase 3** | ICMP 采集器：raw socket、多包统计、直方图 | Phase 2 |
| **Phase 4** | DNS 采集器：多记录类型、自定义 resolver | Phase 2 |
| **Phase 5** | SNMP 采集器：per-target 隔离、标量+表、并发控制 | Phase 2 |
| **Phase 6** | MCP Server：Streamable HTTP 初始化、API Key 中间件 | Phase 1 |
| **Phase 7** | MCP Tools：查询工具 + 管理工具实现 | Phase 6 + Phase 3/4/5 |
| **Phase 8** | 集成测试、跨平台构建、文档 | 全部 |

#### 10. 相比 Categraf 的改进

| 维度 | Categraf | probe-agent |
|------|----------|-------------|
| **ICMP** | 双包/单包，无统计 | 多包突发，RTT 直方图/p50/p90/p99/jitter/stddev/丢包率/连续丢包 |
| **SNMP 隔离** | 目标共享连接，单点超时阻塞全部 | 每目标独立 goroutine + 独立超时/重试，优雅降级 |
| **采集颗粒度** | 全局 interval | 每目标独立 interval（1s ~ 5min） |
| **DNS** | 仅 A 记录 | A/AAAA/MX/NS/CNAME/TXT + 指定 DNS 服务器 |
| **VM 推送** | 依赖 Categraf writers | 内置批处理 + 环形缓冲 + 指数退避重试 |

    ICMP    ICMPConfig
    SNMP    SNMPConfig
    DNS     DNSConfig
}

func (c *Config) Watch(ctx context.Context, onReload func(*Config)) { ... }
```

```go

```

### Main Entry

```go
func main() {
    // Parse flags

}
```

```go
// internal/config/config.go

package config

type Config struct {
    Global  GlobalConfig  `yaml:"-"`
    ICMP    ICMPConfig    `yaml:"-"`
    SNMP    SNMPConfig    `yaml:"-"`
    DNS     DNSConfig     `yaml:"-"`
}

func Load(configDir string) (*Config, error) {
    // Load each file independently
}
```

For global config:
```go
type GlobalConfig struct {
    Concurrency     int           `yaml:"concurrency"`
    LogLevel        string        `yaml:"log_level"`
    VictoriaMetrics VMConfig      `yaml:"victoria_metrics"`
    MCPServer       MCPConfig     `yaml:"mcp_server"`
}

type VMConfig struct {
    PushURL      string        `yaml:"push_url"`
    BatchSize    int           `yaml:"batch_size"`
    FlushInterval time.Duration `yaml:"flush_interval"`
    BufferSize   int           `yaml:"buffer_size"`
    Retry        RetryConfig   `yaml:"retry"`
}

type MCPConfig struct {
    Enabled  bool       `yaml:"enabled"`
    Listen   string     `yaml:"listen"`
    Auth     AuthConfig `yaml:"auth"`
}

type AuthConfig struct {
    Type string   `yaml:"type"`
    Keys []string `yaml:"keys"`
}
```

For ICMP config:
```go
type ICMPTarget struct {
    Host     string            `yaml:"host"`
    Interval time.Duration     `yaml:"interval"`
    Count    int               `yaml:"count"`
    Timeout  time.Duration     `yaml:"timeout"`
    Labels   map[string]string `yaml:"labels"`
}

type ICMPConfig struct {
    HistogramBucketsMs []int        `yaml:"histogram_buckets_ms"`
    Targets            []ICMPTarget `yaml:"targets"`
}
```

For SNMP config:
```go
type SNMPDefaults struct {
    Version   string        `yaml:"version"`
    Community string        `yaml:"community"`
    Port      int           `yaml:"port"`
    Timeout   time.Duration `yaml:"timeout"`
    Retries   int           `yaml:"retries"`
}

type SNMPTableMetric struct {
    OID  string `yaml:"oid"`
    Name string `yaml:"name"`
    Type string `yaml:"type"`
}

type SNMPTable struct {
    OID     string           `yaml:"oid"`
    Index   string           `yaml:"index"`
    Tag     string           `yaml:"tag"`
    Metrics []SNMPTableMetric `yaml:"metrics"`
}

type SNMPTarget struct {
    Host     string            `yaml:"host"`
    Interval time.Duration     `yaml:"interval"`
    Timeout  time.Duration     `yaml:"timeout"`
    Labels   map[string]string `yaml:"labels"`
    OIDs     SNMPOIDs          `yaml:"oids"`
}

type SNMPOIDs struct {
    Scalar  []string    `yaml:"scalar"`
    Tables  []SNMPTable `yaml:"tables"`
}

type SNMPConfig struct {
    Defaults SNMPDefaults `yaml:"defaults"`
    Targets  []SNMPTarget `yaml:"targets"`
}
```

For DNS config:
```go
type DNSTarget struct {
    Domain     string            `yaml:"domain"`
    Server     string            `yaml:"server"`
    RecordType string            `yaml:"record_type"`
    Interval   time.Duration     `yaml:"interval"`
    Labels     map[string]string `yaml:"labels"`
}

type DNSConfig struct {
    Targets []DNSTarget `yaml:"targets"`
}
```

Now let me think about the config loading approach. I want each file to be loaded independently and merged into one Config struct.

func Load(configDir string) (*Config, error) {
    cfg := &Config{

        },
    }

    if err := loadYAML(filepath.Join(configDir, "global.yaml"), &g); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    cfg.Global = g
    
    // ... similar for ICMP, SNMP, DNS
}
```

进入Phase 2

The user wants to proceed with Phase 2 - the Metric Pipeline. This includes:

1. `internal/metrics/metric.go` - Metric structure definition
2. `internal/metrics/histogram.go` - Histogram bucket calculation
3. `internal/metrics/registry.go` - Metric registry with latest value cache
4. `internal/output/output.go` - Output interface
5. `internal/output/vmpush.go` - VictoriaMetrics HTTP push
6. `internal/output/buffer.go` - Ring buffer
7. `internal/output/batch.go` - Batch processing logic

Let me design the metric pipeline architecture:

### Metric Structure

```go
// A metric point with labels, timestamp, and value
type Metric struct {
    Name      string
    Labels    map[string]string  // sorted for consistency
    Value     float64
    Timestamp time.Time
    Type      MetricType  // gauge, counter, histogram
}
```

### Histogram

```go
type Histogram struct {
    Buckets []float64
    Counts  []uint64
    Count   uint64
    Sum     float64
}
```

### Registry

Stores the latest metric values so MCP can query them.

```go
type Registry struct {
    mu sync.RWMutex
    metrics map[string][]Metric  // key = target name or similar
}
```

### Output Pipeline

```
Collector → channel(Metric) → Batcher → buffer → VM Pusher
```

### Buffer (Ring Buffer)

```go
type RingBuffer struct {
    mu     sync.Mutex
    data   []*Metric
    head   int
    tail   int
    count  int
    size   int
}
```

### Batcher

Collects metrics from channel into batches, flushes on size or interval.

### VM Push

Uses HTTP POST to `/api/v1/import/prometheus` with Prometheus text format.

Let me implement all of this now.

下一个阶段是 **Phase 3: ICMP 采集器**，继续推进？
是的

The user wants to proceed with Phase 3: ICMP Collector. Let me implement the ICMP collector module.

Phase 3 includes:
1. `internal/collector/collector.go` - Collector interface and manager
2. `internal/collector/icmp.go` - ICMP collector implementation

Key design:
- Each target runs in its own goroutine
- Uses `golang.org/x/net/icmp` and `golang.org/x/net/ipv4` for raw socket ICMP
- Each probe sends `count` packets, records RTT for each
- Calculates: min/max/avg/stddev, jitter, packet loss, consecutive loss
- Outputs to metric pipeline

Let me think about the ICMP implementation:

For ICMP echo on Windows, `golang.org/x/net/icmp` works differently than on Linux. On Windows, raw sockets require admin privileges, but since the user said they can run as root (admin on Windows), this should work.

Actually, on Windows, Go's `net.Dial("ip4:icmp", ...)` or `net.ListenPacket("ip4:icmp", ...)` requires admin privileges and has some limitations. Let me think about this...

Actually, Go's `golang.org/x/net/icmp` package provides cross-platform ICMP support. On Windows, it uses the `ip4:icmp` protocol, which requires Administrator privileges.

But there's a simpler approach: we can use `golang.org/x/net/icmp` with `net.Dial("ip4:icmp", ...)` on Windows. However, Windows has some quirks with ICMP.

Actually, the most reliable cross-platform approach would be to use `golang.org/x/net/icmp` which handles the platform-specific details:

```go
// Listen to ICMP
c, err := icmp.ListenPacket("ip4:icmp", "0.0.0.0")
```

But on Windows, this requires Administrator privileges. The user confirmed they can run as root, so this should be fine.

Let me also think about the collector manager structure:

```go
type Manager struct {
    collectors []Collector
    // ...
}

type Collector interface {
    Name() string
    Start(ctx context.Context, pipeline *output.Pipeline) error
    Stop() error
}
```

For ICMP specifically:

```go
type ICMPCollector struct {
    targets  []config.ICMPTarget
    pipelines []*icmpTargetRunner
    // ...
}

type icmpTargetRunner struct {
    target   config.ICMPTarget
    buckets  []float64
    hist     *metrics.Histogram
    // stats
}
```

The ICMP flow:
1. For each target, create a goroutine
2. Each interval, send `count` ICMP echo requests
3. Receive replies and record RTT
4. Calculate statistics
5. Submit metrics to pipeline

Let me think about the actual ICMP implementation more carefully.

For sending ICMP echo requests:
1. Create a raw ICMP socket: `icmp.ListenPacket("ip4:icmp", "0.0.0.0")`
2. Create an echo message: `icmp.Message{Type: ipv4.ICMPTypeEcho, Code: 0, Body: &icmp.Echo{ID: os.Getpid()&0xffff, Seq: seq, Data: []byte("")}}`
3. Marshal and send to target
4. Read response with timeout
5. Calculate RTT

For each probe cycle:
```go
func (r *icmpTargetRunner) probe() {
    conn, err := icmp.ListenPacket("ip4:icmp", "0.0.0.0")
    defer conn.Close()
    
    conn.SetDeadline(time.Now().Add(timeout))
    
    var rtts []time.Duration
    var loss int
    
    for i := 0; i < count; i++ {
        start := time.Now()
        // Send echo
        msg := icmp.Message{...}
        wb, _ := msg.Marshal(nil)
        conn.WriteTo(wb, &net.IPAddr{IP: net.ParseIP(target.Host)})
        
        // Receive reply
        rb := make([]byte, 1500)
        n, peer, err := conn.ReadFrom(rb)
        elapsed := time.Since(start)
        
        if err != nil {
            loss++
            continue
        }
        
        // Parse and verify
        rm, err := icmp.ParseMessage(1, rb[:n])
        if rm.Type == ipv4.ICMPTypeEchoReply {
            rtts = append(rtts, elapsed)
        }
    }
    
    // Calculate stats
    // Submit to pipeline
}
```

Wait, this approach has a problem. If we use `SetDeadline` for the entire connection, we can't distinguish between a slow response and a timeout. Let me think about this differently.

A better approach:
1. Send all `count` packets quickly
2. Then read responses in a loop with a short timeout
3. Match responses to sent packets by sequence number
4. Calculate RTT for matched responses

Actually, the simplest approach is:
1. For each of the `count` packets:
   a. Send the packet
   b. Read the reply (with per-packet timeout)
   c. If received, record RTT
   d. If timeout, increment loss counter

But this is sequential and might be slow for large counts. A better approach would be to send all packets first, then read all responses with a shared timeout.

Actually, the most accurate approach for measuring RTT is the ping approach:
1. Send packet, record send time
2. Read reply, record receive time
3. RTT = receive - send

This requires using unique sequence numbers per packet. Let me implement this properly.

Actually, let me simplify. For a monitoring tool, the sequential approach (send one, wait, send next) is perfectly fine. The user configured `count: 5` typically, so the total probe time would be about 5 * timeout, which is manageable.

But a better approach (what `ping` does) is to send all packets, then read responses as they come:

```go
// Send phase
sendTimes := make([]time.Time, count)
for i := 0; i < count; i++ {
    msg := icmp.Message{
        Type: ipv4.ICMPTypeEcho,
        Code: 0,
        Body: &icmp.Echo{
            ID:   id,
            Seq:  i,
            Data: []byte{},
        },
    }
    wb, _ := msg.Marshal(nil)
    conn.WriteTo(wb, addr)
    sendTimes[i] = time.Now()
}

// Receive phase - read until timeout
conn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(timeout))
rtts := make([]float64, 0, count)
lost := 0

for i := 0; i < count; i++ {
    rb := make([]byte, 1500)
    n, _, err := conn.ReadFrom(rb)
    if err != nil {
        // timeout, rest are lost
        lost += count - i
        break
    }
    
    rm, _ := icmp.ParseMessage(1, rb[:n])
    if echo, ok := rm.Body.(*icmp.Echo); ok {
        if echo.ID == id && echo.Seq < count {
            rtt := time.Since(sendTimes[echo.Seq]).Seconds() * 1000
            rtts = append(rtts, rtt)
        }
    } else {
        // non-echo-reply packet, try again (but limit retries)
        continue
    }
}
```

This is the standard ping approach. Let me implement it this way.

Now, for the collector manager, I need a way to:
1. Start all collectors
2. Stop all collectors
3. Each collector manages its own targets

Let me design this:

```go
// internal/collector/collector.go
type Collector interface {
    Name() string
    Start(ctx context.Context, pipeline *output.Pipeline) error
    Stop() error
}

type Manager struct {
    collectors []Collector
}
```

For ICMP:
```go

```

func idFromConn(conn *icmp.PacketConn) int {

**ICMP 采集逻辑：** 每目标独立 goroutine → 按 interval 突发发包 → 接收回复 → 计算 RTT 统计 → 生成指标（min/max/avg/stddev/jitter/loss/histogram）→ 提交 Pipeline

Phase 4

The user wants Phase 4 - the DNS collector. Let me implement it.

DNS collector design:
- Use Go's `net.Resolver` with custom dial to specify DNS server
- Support record types: A, AAAA, MX, NS, CNAME, TXT
- Each target runs in its own goroutine (similar pattern to ICMP)
- Metrics:
  - `dns_lookup_duration_seconds{domain,server,record_type}`
  - `dns_up{domain,server}`
  - `dns_response_code` (NOERROR, NXDOMAIN, SERVFAIL, etc.)
  - `dns_answer_count`

For the DNS resolver:
- Create a custom `net.Resolver` with a `Dial` function that connects to the specified DNS server
- Use `LookupHost`, `LookupMX`, `LookupNS`, `LookupCNAME`, `LookupTXT` depending on record type
- Measure the duration

Let me think about the implementation:

```go
type DNSCollector struct {
    cfg     config.DNSConfig
    runners []*dnsRunner
    logger  *zap.Logger
}

type dnsRunner struct {
    target config.DNSTarget
    resolver *net.Resolver
    logger *zap.Logger
}
```

For the resolver:
```go
r := &net.Resolver{
    PreferGo: true,
    Dial: func(ctx context.Context, network, address string) (net.Conn, error) {
        d := net.Dialer{}
        return d.DialContext(ctx, network, target.Server + ":53")
    },
}
```

For the record type dispatch:
```go
func (r *dnsRunner) lookup(ctx context.Context) (int, string, error) {
    switch strings.ToUpper(r.target.RecordType) {
    case "A":
        ips, err := r.resolver.LookupHost(ctx, r.target.Domain)
        return len(ips), "NOERROR", err
    case "AAAA":
        ips, err := r.resolver.LookupHost(ctx, r.target.Domain)
        // filter for IPv6...
        return len(ips), "NOERROR", err
    case "MX":
        mx, err := r.resolver.LookupMX(ctx, r.target.Domain)
        return len(mx), "NOERROR", err
    case "NS":
        ns, err := r.resolver.LookupNS(ctx, r.target.Domain)
        return len(ns), "NOERROR", err
    case "CNAME":
        cname, err := r.resolver.LookupCNAME(ctx, r.target.Domain)
        if cname != "" { return 1, "NOERROR", err }
        return 0, "NOERROR", err
    case "TXT":
        txt, err := r.resolver.LookupTXT(ctx, r.target.Domain)
        return len(txt), "NOERROR", err
    }
}
```

But `net.LookupHost` returns both A and AAAA records. For specific A vs AAAA, I should use `net.Resolver.LookupIPAddr` with network "ip4" or "ip6".

Actually, `net.Resolver.LookupHost` returns both IPv4 and IPv6. For separating them:
- Use `net.Resolver.LookupIPAddr(ctx, "ip4", domain)` for A records
- Use `net.Resolver.LookupIPAddr(ctx, "ip6", domain)` for AAAA records

For error handling in DNS lookups, I need to detect the type of error:
- `net.DNSError` has `IsNotFound`, `IsTimeout`, `Err` fields
- Timeout → SERVFAIL or TIMEOUT
- NXDOMAIN → not found

Let me implement this now.

Actually, let me simplify the DNS error handling. The `net.DNSError` type has:
- `IsNotFound` - true if the DNS lookup was successful but no records found (NXDOMAIN or NODATA)
- `IsTimeout` - true if the lookup timed out
- `Err` - the underlying error

```go
func dnsErrorCode(err error) string {
    if err == nil {
        return "NOERROR"
    }
    var dnsErr *net.DNSError
    if errors.As(err, &dnsErr) {
        if dnsErr.IsTimeout {
            return "TIMEOUT"
        }
        if dnsErr.IsNotFound {
            return "NXDOMAIN"
        }
    }
    return "SERVFAIL"
}
```

Phase 4 完成。DNS 采集器 3 个目标初始化成功。

```
internal/collector/dns.go
├── DNSCollector   → 多目标管理
└── dnsRunner      → 单目标采集
    ├── 自定义 resolver（指定 DNS 服务器 :53）
    ├── 支持 A/AAAA/MX/NS/CNAME/TXT
    ├── dns_up / dns_lookup_duration_seconds / dns_answer_count / dns_response{code}
    └── 错误码分类：NOERROR / NXDOMAIN / TIMEOUT / SERVFAIL
```

**Phase 5: SNMP 采集器** 推进？
是的

The user wants to proceed with Phase 5 - the SNMP collector. Let me implement it.

SNMP collector design:
- Use `github.com/gosnmp/gosnmp`
- Each target runs in its own goroutine (isolated connection, timeout, retry)
- Support scalar OIDs (Get) and table OIDs (Walk/BulkWalk)
- Each target has independent timeout - one failure doesn't affect others
- Use errgroup + semaphore for concurrency control
- Metrics:
  - Scalar OIDs → `snmp_{name}{target,oid}`
  - Table OIDs → `snmp_{name}{target,index,tag}` (e.g., `snmp_if_in_octets{target="192.168.1.1",ifIndex="1",ifDescr="Gig0/1"}`)
  - `snmp_up{target}` = 1/0

Let me think about the implementation:

```go
type SNMPCollector struct {
    cfg     config.SNMPConfig
    runners []*snmpRunner
    logger  *zap.Logger
}

type snmpRunner struct {
    target config.SNMPTarget
    defaults config.SNMPDefaults
    logger *zap.Logger
}
```

For gosnmp usage:
```go
func (r *snmpRunner) probe() {
    g := &gosnmp.GoSNMP{
        Target:    r.target.Host,
        Port:      port,
        Community: community,
        Version:   version,
        Timeout:   timeout,
        Retries:   retries,
    }
    
    err := g.Connect()
    if err != nil {
        // snmp_up = 0
        return
    }
    defer g.Conn.Close()
    
    // Scalar OIDs
    for _, oid := range r.target.OIDs.Scalar {
        result, err := g.Get([]string{oid})
        if err == nil && len(result.Variables) > 0 {
            // process result
        }
    }
    
    // Table OIDs - walk
    for _, table := range r.target.OIDs.Tables {
        // Walk the table index first
        // Then walk each metric OID
    }
}
```

For the version, I need to map strings to gosnmp version constants:
```go
func snmpVersion(v string) gosnmp.SnmpVersion {
    switch v {
    case "1":
        return gosnmp.Version1
    case "2c":
        return gosnmp.Version2c
    case "3":
        return gosnmp.Version3
    default:
        return gosnmp.Version2c
    }
}
```

For table walking, the approach is:
1. Walk the tag OID (e.g., ifDescr) to get the mapping of index → name
2. Walk each metric OID (e.g., ifInOctets) and use the index to look up the tag
3. Emit metrics with index and tag as labels

Actually, a simpler approach for table walking:
1. For each metric in a table, walk the OID
2. Each result contains an OID that includes the index
3. Extract the index from the OID suffix
4. Look up the tag (if we also walked it)
5. Emit metric with index and tag labels

Let me implement this step by step.

For gosnmp.Walk/WalkAll, the callback receives each PDUElement. The OID includes the index as the last component.

Example: Walking `1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.10` (ifInOctets) would return:
- `1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.10.1` → value for ifIndex=1
- `1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.10.2` → value for ifIndex=2
etc.

The index is the last OID component. We extract it and use it to look up the tag name from our tag mapping.

For the tag lookup, we need to walk the tag OID first:
- `1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.2` (ifDescr) → `1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.2.1` = "Gig0/1", `1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.2.2` = "Gig0/2"

So the algorithm is:
1. For each table, walk the tag OID first to build a map of index → tag value
2. Then walk each metric OID, extract the index, look up the tag, emit the metric

Let me think about how gosnmp's Walk works. Actually, I should use `gosnmp.Walk` or `gosnmp.BulkWalk` which takes a callback.

gosnmp provides:
- `g.Walk(oid, walkFn)` - SNMP walk, calls walkFn for each result
- `g.BulkWalk(oid, walkFn)` - SNMP bulk walk (more efficient)
- `g.Get(oids)` - SNMP get

The walkFn is `gosnmp.WalkFunc` which is `func(pdu gosnmp.SnmpPDU) error`.

Each SnmpPDU has:
- `Name` - the full OID (string)
- `Type` - the SNMP type (gosnmp.Asn1BER)
- `Value` - the value (interface{})

For SNMP value conversion:
- Integer/Integer32 → int
- Counter32/Counter64 → uint/uint64
- Gauge32 → uint
- OctetString → string (for tags)
- IPAddress → string
- ObjectIdentifier → string
- TimeTicks → uint32

Let me write the implementation now.

Actually, I need to be careful about one thing: the user's remote system where this will be deployed is likely Linux, not Windows. The SNMP library should work cross-platform though.

Let me write the code.

Also there's a `g.Conn.Close()` call - gosnmp's GoSNMP doesn't have a Conn field. It has a `g.Conn` which is the underlying connection. Let me check the gosnmp API...

Actually, in gosnmp, after calling `g.Connect()`, the connection is stored in `g.Conn`. But `g.Conn` is of type `net.Conn` which has a `Close()` method. However, the preferred way to close is to call `g.Conn.Close()` or simply `defer g.Conn.Close()`. But actually gosnmp has a `Close()` method on the GoSNMP struct itself.

Wait, let me check: the `*gosnmp.GoSNMP` has a Conn field of type `net.Conn`. So `g.Conn.Close()` should work. But actually, looking at recent versions, they might have removed direct access to `Conn`. Let me use `g.Conn.Close()` for now and see if it builds.

下个阶段是 **Phase 6: MCP Server (Streamable HTTP + API Key 鉴权)**，继续？
是的

Now I need to implement Phase 6 - MCP Server with Streamable HTTP transport and API Key authentication.

The MCP server will:
1. Use `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` library
2. Implement Streamable HTTP transport
3. API Key authentication middleware
4. Register tools for querying and managing monitoring targets

Let me design the MCP module:

```
internal/mcp/
├── server.go       - Initialize MCPServer + StreamableHTTPServer
├── auth.go         - API Key middleware
├── tools_setup.go  - Tool registration
├── tools_query.go  - Query tool implementations
└── tools_manage.go - Management tool implementations
```

For the MCP tools, I need to think about what information to expose:
1. `get_targets` - List all targets with their status
2. `get_metrics` - Query specific metrics for a target
3. `get_icmp_stats` - ICMP-specific details
4. `get_snmp_data` - SNMP data for a device
5. `add_target` - Add a new target
6. `remove_target` - Remove a target
7. `update_target` - Update a target config
8. `reload_config` - Trigger config reload

For Phase 6, I'll focus on:
- Setting up the MCP server with Streamable HTTP
- API Key middleware
- Basic tool infrastructure
- Registering the query tools (get_targets, get_metrics)

The manage tools (add/remove/update/reload) will be completed in Phase 7.

Let me also think about how the MCP server shares data with the collectors. The MCP needs access to:
1. The metrics registry (to query latest values)
2. The config (to list targets)
3. The config reload function

I'll pass these as dependencies when creating the MCP server.

Let me look at the current main.go to understand what I need to wire up.

The `mark3labs/mcp-go` library provides:
- `server.NewMCPServer(name, version)` - Creates an MCP server
- `mcpServer.AddTool(tool)` - Registers a tool
- `server.NewStreamableHTTPServer(mcpServer, opts...)` - Creates an HTTP transport
- `server.WithEndpointPath(path)` - Sets the endpoint path
- `server.WithStateful(enable)` - Enables stateful sessions

The `StreamableHTTPServer` is an `http.Handler`, so I can wrap it with auth middleware.

For the MCP tools, the API is:
```go
server.NewTool(
    "tool_name",
    server.WithDescription("..."),
    server.WithStringProperty("param1", "description", true/false),
    // ... more properties
)
```

Tool handlers are:
```go
mcpServer.AddTool(tool, func(ctx context.Context, req mcp.CallToolRequest) (*mcp.CallToolResult, error) {
    // ...
    return mcp.NewToolResultText("result"), nil
})
```

Let me implement this now.

For the auth middleware, I'll wrap the StreamableHTTPServer:
```go
func AuthMiddleware(next http.Handler, keys []string) http.Handler {
    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        // Check Authorization header
        // Validate Bearer token
        // Return 401 if invalid
    })
}
```

Let me think about the MCP module structure more carefully:

```go
// internal/mcp/server.go
type Server struct {
    mcpServer  *mcp.MCPServer
    httpServer *server.StreamableHTTPServer
    registry   *metrics.Registry
    config     *config.Config
    logger     *zap.Logger
}

func New(cfg config.MCPConfig, reg *metrics.Registry, appCfg *config.Config, logger *zap.Logger) (*Server, error) {
    s := &Server{
        registry: reg,
        config:   appCfg,
        logger:   logger.Named("mcp"),
    }
    
    mcpSrv := server.NewMCPServer("probe-agent", "1.0.0")
    
    // Register tools
    s.registerTools(mcpSrv)
    
    // Create Streamable HTTP transport
    httpSrv := server.NewStreamableHTTPServer(mcpSrv,
        server.WithEndpointPath("/mcp"),
    )
    
    s.httpServer = httpSrv
    return s, nil
}

func (s *Server) Handler() http.Handler {
    handler := s.httpServer
    if len(s.cfg.Auth.Keys) > 0 && s.cfg.Auth.Type == "api_key" {
        handler = authMiddleware(handler, s.cfg.Auth.Keys)
    }
    return handler
}

func (s *Server) Start(addr string) error {
    handler := s.Handler()
    // Start HTTP server with handler
}
```

Now let me think about what tools to register:

1. `get_targets`:
   - Parameters: module (optional - "icmp", "snmp", "dns")
   - Returns: list of targets with their status (from registry)
   
2. `get_metrics`:
   - Parameters: target (required), module (optional)
   - Returns: latest metrics for that target
   
3. `get_icmp_stats`:
   - Parameters: target (required)
   - Returns: ICMP-specific statistics
   
4. `get_snmp_data`:

For `remove_target`:

Let me compile the summary.

## Goal
- Build a custom Go monitoring agent (`probe-agent`) to replace Categraf for ICMP/DNS/SNMP probing with per-target isolation and fine-grained ICMP statistics, outputting to VictoriaMetrics with MCP Streamable HTTP Server for AI-assistant interaction.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Deployment: binary directly, run as root
- ICMP: multi-packet burst, RTT histogram (p50/p90/p99), jitter, stddev, packet loss, consecutive loss
- SNMP: 50-500 targets, per-target independent goroutine + timeout, raw numeric OIDs (no MIB names), table walk with index/tag labels
- DNS: A/AAAA/MX/NS/CNAME/TXT, custom DNS server per target
- Output: VictoriaMetrics `/api/v1/import/prometheus` (Prometheus text format push)
- Config: YAML, split by module (`config/{global,icmp,snmp,dns}.yaml`)
- MCP: Streamable HTTP transport (`/mcp` endpoint), API Key auth (`Authorization: Bearer <key>`), query + management tools
- Dependencies: `mark3labs/mcp-go` (v0.54.1), `golang.org/x/net/icmp+ipv4`, `gosnmp/gosnmp`, `yaml.v3`, `zap`
- Go version: 1.26.4, module path `probe-agent`

## Progress
### Done
- Phase 1: Project skeleton, split config loading (`internal/config/`), zap logger, SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handling, `cmd/probe-agent/main.go`
- Phase 2: Metric pipeline (`internal/metrics/` — Metric struct, Histogram with bucket/count/sum, Registry with RWMutex; `internal/output/` — RingBuffer, Batcher (Prometheus text format), VMPusher (exponential backoff retry), Pipeline orchestrator)
- Phase 3: ICMP collector (`internal/collector/icmp.go`): raw `icmp.ListenPacket`, multi-packet send-receive, RTT min/max/avg/stddev/jitter, packet loss ratio, consecutive loss counter, configurable histogram buckets
- Phase 4: DNS collector (`internal/collector/dns.go`): `net.Resolver` with custom `Dial` to specified DNS server:53, record types A/AAAA/MX/NS/CNAME/TXT, `dns_up/dns_lookup_duration_seconds/dns_answer_count/dns_response{code}` with code NOERROR/NXDOMAIN/TIMEOUT/SERVFAIL
- Phase 5: SNMP collector (`internal/collector/snmp.go`): `gosnmp` per-target isolated Connect/Close, scalar OIDs via `Get`, table OIDs via `Walk` with index extraction and tag lookup, `snmp_up` health metric
- Build & vet pass for all phases, test run verifies all collectors initialize (2 ICMP + 3 DNS + 2 SNMP targets)

### In Progress
- Phase 6: MCP Server — code written (`internal/mcp/server.go`, `auth.go`, `tools_query.go`) but **not yet wired into `main.go`**; MCP module imports conflict resolved (external mcp-go imported as `mcpcore`); need to add mcpSrv.Start/Stop to main.go and run `go mod tidy` + build

### Blocked
- (none)

## Key Decisions
- MCP transport: Streamable HTTP (not stdio, not SSE), via `mark3labs/mcp-go` `StreamableHTTPServer` with `/mcp` endpoint
- MCP auth: API Key via `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header, HTTP middleware wrapping `StreamableHTTPServer`, support multiple concurrent keys for rotation
- ICMP: use `os.Getpid() & 0xffff` as ICMP Echo ID (not `conn.IPv4PacketConn().ID()` which is unreliable on Windows)
- DNS: Use `LookupNetIP("ip4", ...)` / `LookupNetIP("ip6", ...)` for A/AAAA respectively (Go 1.24+ API)
- Config: each YAML file loaded independently, missing files silently skipped
- SNMP port type: `uint16` in config (to match `gosnmp.GoSNMP.Port`)

## Next Steps
1. Wire MCP server into `main.go` (create mcp Server from `mcp.New()` with deps, call `Start()`/`Stop()`)
2. Run `go mod tidy` + `go build` to verify MCP compiles
3. Proceed to Phase 7: MCP management tools (`add_target`/`remove_target`/`update_target`/`reload_config`)
4. Phase 8: Integration tests and cross-platform build

## Critical Context
- Go installed via scoop: `C:\Users\ZeroYe\scoop\apps\go\1.26.4`
- Go proxy in China: `$env:GOPROXY="https://goproxy.cn,direct"`
- Current MCP implementation provides 2 tools: `get_targets(module?)` and `get_metrics(target, module?)`
- `internal/mcp/auth.go` defines `APIKeyAuth` with `UpdateKeys()` for hot-reload, `AuthMiddleware` returns 401 JSON on invalid/missing key
- `checkModuleUp` in tools_query.go scans metric list for `_up` metric with value 0 to determine target health

## Relevant Files
- `cmd/probe-agent/main.go`: binary entry point, missing MCP server wiring (must be added)
- `internal/config/config.go`: root config loading from `config/` directory
- `internal/config/global.go`: `GlobalConfig` with `VMConfig`, `MCPConfig`, `AuthConfig`
- `internal/config/icmp.go`: `ICMPConfig` with `Targets` and `HistogramBucketsMs`
- `internal/config/snmp.go`: `SNMPConfig` with `Defaults` (version/community/port/timeout/retries) and `Targets` (host/interval/oids.scalar/tables)
- `internal/config/dns.go`: `DNSConfig` with `Targets` (domain/server/record_type/interval)
- `internal/collector/collector.go`: `Collector` interface + `Manager` (Add/Start/Stop)
- `internal/collector/icmp.go`: ICMP collector, raw socket, multi-packet probe, histogram
- `internal/collector/dns.go`: DNS collector, custom resolver, A/AAAA/MX/NS/CNAME/TXT
- `internal/collector/snmp.go`: SNMP collector, gosnmp per-target, scalar Get + table Walk
- `internal/metrics/metric.go`: `Metric` struct, `MetricType`, `MetricPool`
- `internal/metrics/histogram.go`: `Histogram` with `Observe`/`Reset`/`Metrics(name, labels, ts)`
- `internal/metrics/registry.go`: `Registry` with RWMutex, `Store`/`Get`/`Keys`/`All`
- `internal/output/output.go`: `Output` interface
- `internal/output/buffer.go`: `RingBuffer` with `Push`/`PopN`/`PushBatch`
- `internal/output/batch.go`: `Batcher` formats Prometheus text, handles sort and flush
- `internal/output/vmpush.go`: `VMPusher` HTTP POST with retry/backoff, flush loop
- `internal/output/pipeline.go`: `Pipeline` orchestrator, `Submit(key, metrics)` channel, `Registry` integration
- `internal/mcp/server.go`: `Server` wrapping `server.NewMCPServer` + `server.NewStreamableHTTPServer`, tool registration, Start/Stop lifecycle
- `internal/mcp/auth.go`: `APIKeyAuth` struct + `AuthMiddleware` `http.Handler` wrapper
- `internal/mcp/tools_query.go`: `get_targets` and `get_metrics` tool definitions + handlers
- `config/global.yaml`: global config with VM push URL, batch size, MCP auth keys
- `config/icmp.yaml`: ICMP targets (8.8.8.8, 114.114.114.114) with interval/count/timeout/labels
- `config/snmp.yaml`: SNMP targets (192.168.1.1, 192.168.1.2) with scalar + table OIDs
- `config/dns.yaml`: DNS targets (example.com, mail.example.com) with server/record_type

func restartCollectors(colMgr *collector.Manager, cfg *config.Config, pipeline *output.Pipeline) error {

	colMgr = collector.NewManager()
	colMgr.Add(collector.NewICMPCollector(cfg.ICMP, logger.Log))
	colMgr.Add(collector.NewDNSCollector(cfg.DNS, logger.Log))

	return colMgr.Start(context.Background(), pipeline)
}

82: 	merge["le"] = "+Inf"
83: 	ms = append(ms, Metric{
84: 		Name:      name + "_bucket",
85: 		Labels:    copyMapLabels(merge),
86: 		Value:     float64(h.Count),
87: 		Timestamp: now,
88: 		Type:      TypeGauge,
89: 	})
90: 
91: 	return ms
92: }

95: 	return strconv.FormatFloat(f, 'f', -1, 64)

101: 	}

1: package output
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"sync"
5: 
6: 	"probe-agent/internal/metrics"
7: )
8: 
9: type RingBuffer struct {
10: 	mu    sync.Mutex
11: 	data  []metrics.Metric
12: 	head  int
13: 	tail  int
14: 	count int
15: 	size  int
16: }
17: 
18: func NewRingBuffer(size int) *RingBuffer {
19: 	if size <= 0 {
20: 		size = 10000
21: 	}
22: 	return &RingBuffer{
23: 		data: make([]metrics.Metric, size),
24: 		size: size,
25: 	}
26: }
27: 
28: func (b *RingBuffer) Push(m metrics.Metric) {
29: 	b.mu.Lock()
30: 	defer b.mu.Unlock()
31: 
32: 	b.data[b.tail] = m
33: 	b.tail = (b.tail + 1) % b.size
34: 	if b.count == b.size {
35: 		b.head = (b.head + 1) % b.size
36: 	} else {
37: 		b.count++
38: 	}
39: }
40: 
41: func (b *RingBuffer) PopN(n int) []metrics.Metric {
42: 	b.mu.Lock()
43: 	defer b.mu.Unlock()
44: 
45: 	if n <= 0 || b.count == 0 {
46: 		return nil
47: 	}
48: 	if n > b.count {
49: 		n = b.count
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	result := make([]metrics.Metric, n)
53: 	for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
54: 		result[i] = b.data[b.head]
55: 		b.head = (b.head + 1) % b.size
56: 	}
57: 	b.count -= n
58: 
59: 	return result
60: }
61: 
62: func (b *RingBuffer) Len() int {
63: 	b.mu.Lock()
64: 	defer b.mu.Unlock()
65: 	return b.count
66: }
67: 
68: func (b *RingBuffer) PushBatch(ms []metrics.Metric) {
69: 	b.mu.Lock()
70: 	defer b.mu.Unlock()
71: 
72: 	for _, m := range ms {
73: 		b.data[b.tail] = m
74: 		b.tail = (b.tail + 1) % b.size
75: 		if b.count == b.size {
76: 			b.head = (b.head + 1) % b.size
77: 		} else {
78: 			b.count++
79: 		}
80: 	}
81: }

(End of file - total 81 lines)

1: package output
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"strconv"
5: 	"strings"
6: 	"sync"
7: 	"time"
8: 
9: 	"probe-agent/internal/metrics"
10: )
11: 
12: type Batcher struct {
13: 	mu       sync.Mutex
14: 	buf      strings.Builder
15: 	count    int
16: 	batchSize int
17: 	interval time.Duration
18: }
19: 
20: func NewBatcher(batchSize int, interval time.Duration) *Batcher {
21: 	return &Batcher{
22: 		batchSize: batchSize,
23: 		interval:  interval,
24: 	}
25: }
26: 
27: func (b *Batcher) Add(ms []metrics.Metric) string {
28: 	b.mu.Lock()
29: 	defer b.mu.Unlock()
30: 
31: 	for _, m := range ms {
32: 		b.writeMetric(m)
33: 	}
34: 
35: 	if b.count >= b.batchSize {
36: 		return b.flush()
37: 	}
38: 	return ""
39: }
40: 
41: func (b *Batcher) Flush() string {
42: 	b.mu.Lock()
43: 	defer b.mu.Unlock()
44: 	return b.flush()
45: }
46: 
47: func (b *Batcher) flush() string {
48: 	if b.count == 0 {
49: 		return ""
50: 	}
51: 	data := b.buf.String()
52: 	b.buf.Reset()
53: 	b.count = 0
54: 	return data
55: }
56: 
57: func (b *Batcher) writeMetric(m metrics.Metric) {
58: 	b.buf.WriteString(m.Name)
59: 
60: 	if len(m.Labels) > 0 {
61: 		b.buf.WriteByte('{')
62: 		first := true
63: 		keys := make([]string, 0, len(m.Labels))
64: 		for k := range m.Labels {
65: 			keys = append(keys, k)
66: 		}
67: 		sortStrings(keys)
68: 		for _, k := range keys {
69: 			if !first {
70: 				b.buf.WriteByte(',')
71: 			}
72: 			b.buf.WriteString(k)
73: 			b.buf.WriteString(`="`)
74: 			b.buf.WriteString(m.Labels[k])
75: 			b.buf.WriteByte('"')
76: 			first = false
77: 		}
78: 		b.buf.WriteByte('}')
79: 	}
80: 
81: 	b.buf.WriteByte(' ')
82: 	b.buf.WriteString(float64Str(m.Value))
83: 	b.buf.WriteByte(' ')
84: 	b.buf.WriteString(strconv.FormatInt(m.Timestamp.Unix(), 10))
85: 	b.buf.WriteByte('\n')
86: 	b.count++
87: }
88: 
89: func float64Str(f float64) string {
90: 	return strconv.FormatFloat(f, 'f', -1, 64)
91: }
92: 
93: func sortStrings(s []string) {
94: 	for i := 0; i < len(s)-1; i++ {
95: 		for j := i + 1; j < len(s); j++ {
96: 			if s[i] > s[j] {
97: 				s[i], s[j] = s[j], s[i]
98: 			}
99: 		}
100: 	}
101: }

package config

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"
)

func TestLoadEmptyDir(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "global.yaml"), []byte("log_level: debug\nvictoria_metrics:\n  push_url: http://vm:8428/api/v1/import/prometheus\n  flush_interval: 10\n  batch_size: 500\nmcp_server:\n  enabled: true\n  listen: :9801\n  auth:\n    type: api_key\n    keys: [test-key]\n"), 0644)

	cfg, err := Load(dir)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Load failed: %v", err)
	}

	if cfg.Global.LogLevel != "debug" {
		t.Errorf("expected log_level debug, got %q", cfg.Global.LogLevel)
	}
	if cfg.Global.VictoriaMetrics.PushURL != "http://vm:8428/api/v1/import/prometheus" {
		t.Errorf("unexpected push_url")
	}
	if !cfg.Global.MCPServer.Enabled {
		t.Errorf("expected mcp enabled")
	}
	if len(cfg.Global.MCPServer.Auth.Keys) != 1 || cfg.Global.MCPServer.Auth.Keys[0] != "test-key" {
		t.Errorf("unexpected auth keys")
	}
}

func TestLoadAllConfigs(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()

	writeYAML(t, dir, "global.yaml", globalYAML)
	writeYAML(t, dir, "icmp.yaml", icmpYAML)
	writeYAML(t, dir, "snmp.yaml", snmpYAML)
	writeYAML(t, dir, "dns.yaml", dnsYAML)

	cfg, err := Load(dir)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Load failed: %v", err)
	}

	if len(cfg.ICMP.Targets) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("icmp: expected 2 targets, got %d", len(cfg.ICMP.Targets))
	}
	if len(cfg.SNMP.Targets) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("snmp: expected 2 targets, got %d", len(cfg.SNMP.Targets))
	}
	if len(cfg.DNS.Targets) != 3 {
		t.Errorf("dns: expected 3 targets, got %d", len(cfg.DNS.Targets))
	}
}

func TestLoadMissingNonGlobal(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	writeYAML(t, dir, "global.yaml", globalYAML)

	cfg, err := Load(dir)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Load failed with missing non-global configs: %v", err)
	}
	if cfg == nil {
		t.Fatal("cfg is nil")
	}
}

func writeYAML(t *testing.T, dir, name, content string) {
	t.Helper()
	if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, name), []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("write %s: %v", name, err)
	}
}

const globalYAML = `log_level: info
victoria_metrics:
  push_url: "http://victoria:8428/api/v1/import/prometheus"
  flush_interval: 10
  batch_size: 500
mcp_server:
  enabled: true
  listen: ":9801"
  auth:
    type: api_key
    keys: ["test-key-1", "test-key-2"]
`

const icmpYAML = `histogram_buckets_ms: [1, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100]
targets:
  - host: "8.8.8.8"
    interval: 60s
    count: 4
    timeout: 5s
    labels:
      region: global
  - host: "114.114.114.114"
    interval: 30s
    count: 2
    timeout: 3s
`

const snmpYAML = `defaults:
  version: "2c"
  community: "public"
  port: 161
  timeout: 5s
  retries: 1
targets:
  - host: "192.168.1.1"
    interval: 60s
    labels:
      role: gw
    oids:
      scalar: ["1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0", "1.3.6.1.2.1.1.5.0"]
      tables:
        - oid: "1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2"
          index: "ifIndex"
          tag: "ifDescr"
          metrics:
            - oid: "1.3.6.1.2.1.2.2.1.10"
              name: "ifInOctets"
              type: "counter"
  - host: "192.168.1.2"
    interval: 120s
`

const dnsYAML = `targets:
  - domain: "example.com"
    server: "8.8.8.8"
    record_type: "A"
    interval: 60s
    labels:
      type: external
  - domain: "example.com"
    server: "8.8.8.8"
    record_type: "AAAA"
    interval: 60s
  - domain: "mail.example.com"
    server: "1.1.1.1"
    record_type: "MX"
    interval: 120s
`

parts.text:
Three failures to fix:

1. **MCP test**: `result.Content[0].Text` - need to check the mcp-go Content type. It might be `Text` field accessed differently. Let me check the mcp-go API.
2. **Config test**: `cannot unmarshal !!int 10 into time.Duration` - YAML doesn't know `10` is meant to be seconds. I need to use `10s` for duration fields.
3. **Batch test**: `Timestamp: time.Unix(0, 0)` is year 1, not timestamp 0. I should use `time.Unix(0, 0)` properly. Actually, `time.Unix(0, 0)` gives the Unix epoch (1970-01-01) which corresponds to Unix timestamp 0. But the test got `-62135596800` which is year 1. Let me check.

Wait, `time.Unix(0, 0)` should be Unix timestamp 0. But the batch test file has:
```go
Labels: map[string]string{"z": "1", "a": "2", "n": "3"},
```
And the Metric has no Timestamp set, so it defaults to `time.Time{}` which is year 1. The test expected timestamp 0 but the actual is `0` (Epoch). Let me fix the test.

Actually no, looking at the output: `m{a="2",n="3",z="1"} 1 -62135596800`. The timestamp -62135596800 is from `time.Time{}` (the zero value, which is year 1). The test expects `0`. I need to set the Timestamp explicitly.

Let me fix all three issues at once.

display.text:
package config

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"
)

func TestLoadEmptyDir(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "global.yaml"), []byte("log_level: debug\nvictoria_metrics:\n  push_url: http://vm:8428/api/v1/import/prometheus\n  flush_interval: 10\n  batch_size: 500\nmcp_server:\n  enabled: true\n  listen: :9801\n  auth:\n    type: api_key\n    keys: [test-key]\n"), 0644)

	cfg, err := Load(dir)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Load failed: %v", err)
	}

	if cfg.Global.LogLevel != "debug" {
		t.Errorf("expected log_level debug, got %q", cfg.Global.LogLevel)
	}
	if cfg.Global.VictoriaMetrics.PushURL != "http://vm:8428/api/v1/import/prometheus" {
		t.Errorf("unexpected push_url")
	}
	if !cfg.Global.MCPServer.Enabled {
		t.Errorf("expected mcp enabled")
	}
	if len(cfg.Global.MCPServer.Auth.Keys) != 1 || cfg.Global.MCPServer.Auth.Keys[0] != "test-key" {
		t.Errorf("unexpected auth keys")
	}
}

func TestLoadAllConfigs(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()

	writeYAML(t, dir, "global.yaml", globalYAML)
	writeYAML(t, dir, "icmp.yaml", icmpYAML)
	writeYAML(t, dir, "snmp.yaml", snmpYAML)
	writeYAML(t, dir, "dns.yaml", dnsYAML)

	cfg, err := Load(dir)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Load failed: %v", err)
	}

	if len(cfg.ICMP.Targets) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("icmp: expected 2 targets, got %d", len(cfg.ICMP.Targets))
	}
	if len(cfg.SNMP.Targets) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("snmp: expected 2 targets, got %d", len(cfg.SNMP.Targets))
	}
	if len(cfg.DNS.Targets) != 3 {
		t.Errorf("dns: expected 3 targets, got %d", len(cfg.DNS.Targets))
	}
}

func TestLoadMissingNonGlobal(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	writeYAML(t, dir, "global.yaml", globalYAML)

	cfg, err := Load(dir)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Load failed with missing non-global configs: %v", err)
	}
	if cfg == nil {
		t.Fatal("cfg is nil")
	}
}

func writeYAML(t *testing.T, dir, name, content string) {
	t.Helper()
	if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, name), []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("write %s: %v", name, err)
	}
}

const globalYAML = `log_level: info

mcp_server:
  enabled: true

    type: api_key

`

All tests pass. But interesting - `TestGetMetricsHandler` says "No metrics found for target: 8.8.8.8" even though we stored metrics with key `icmp:8.8.8.8`. That's because the `get_metrics` handler doesn't recognize the key prefix `icmp:` as matching target `8.8.8.8`.

Wait, looking at the test, the Registry stores `icmp:8.8.8.8` as a key, and then the `get_metrics` handler tries to find metrics for target `8.8.8.8`. The handler should scan all keys for the target. Let me check the handler code.

Actually, looking at the tools_query.go handler, the `get_metrics` handler iterates over all registry keys and checks if the key contains the target. But it uses `module:host` format for keys. The target `8.8.8.8` should match `icmp:8.8.8.8` via `strings.Contains`. Let me re-check the handler code.

65: 			})

101: func (s *Server) handleGetMetrics(ctx context.Context, req mcpcore.CallToolRequest) (*mcpcore.CallToolResult, error) {
102: 	target := argString(req, "target")
103: 	module := argString(req, "module")
104: 
105: 	if target == "" {
106: 		return mcpcore.NewToolResultError("target is required"), nil
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	keys := s.findMetricKeys(target, module)
110: 
111: 	if len(keys) == 0 {
112: 		return mcpcore.NewToolResultText(fmt.Sprintf("No metrics found for target: %s", target)), nil
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	var b strings.Builder
116: 	b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Metrics for %s:\n\n", target))
117: 
118: 	for _, key := range keys {
119: 		ms := s.deps.Registry.Get(key)
120: 		if len(ms) == 0 {
121: 			continue
122: 		}
123: 		b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("--- %s ---\n", key))
124: 		for _, m := range ms {
125: 			labelStr := fmtLabels(m.Labels)
126: 			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  %s%s = %.3f\n", m.Name, labelStr, m.Value))
127: 		}
128: 		b.WriteString("\n")
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	return mcpcore.NewToolResultText(b.String()), nil
132: }
133: 
134: func (s *Server) findMetricKeys(target, module string) []string {
135: 	var allKeys []string
136: 
137: 	if module == "" || module == "icmp" {
138: 		if key := "icmp/" + target; s.deps.Registry.Get(key) != nil {
139: 			allKeys = append(allKeys, key)
140: 		}
141: 	}
142: 
143: 	if module == "" || module == "dns" {
144: 		for _, k := range s.deps.Registry.Keys() {
145: 			if strings.HasPrefix(k, "dns/") && strings.Contains(k, target) {
146: 				allKeys = append(allKeys, k)
147: 			}
148: 		}
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	if module == "" || module == "snmp" {
152: 		if key := "snmp/" + target; s.deps.Registry.Get(key) != nil {
153: 			allKeys = append(allKeys, key)
154: 		}
155: 	}
156: 
157: 	return allKeys
158: }
159: 
160: func checkModuleUp(registry *metrics.Registry, key string) bool {
161: 	ms := registry.Get(key)
162: 	if ms == nil {
163: 		return true
164: 	}
165: 	for _, m := range ms {
166: 		if strings.HasSuffix(m.Name, "_up") && m.Value == 0 {
167: 			return false
168: 		}
169: 	}
170: 	return true
171: }
172: 
173: func mergeMapLabels(labels map[string]string, kv ...string) map[string]string {
174: 	m := make(map[string]string, len(labels)+len(kv)/2)
175: 	for k, v := range labels {
176: 		m[k] = v
177: 	}
178: 	for i := 0; i < len(kv)-1; i += 2 {
179: 		m[kv[i]] = kv[i+1]
180: 	}
181: 	return m
182: }
183: 
184: func fmtLabels(labels map[string]string) string {
185: 	if len(labels) == 0 {
186: 		return ""
187: 	}
188: 	data, err := json.Marshal(labels)
189: 	if err != nil {
190: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%v", labels)
191: 	}
192: 	return string(data)
193: }

So collectors use:
- ICMP: `icmp/<host>` (e.g. `icmp/8.8.8.8`)
- DNS: `dns/<domain>|<server>` (e.g. `dns/example.com|8.8.8.8`)
- SNMP: `snmp/<host>` (e.g. `snmp/192.168.1.1`)

The handler uses the same format, so it works. The test just needs to use `/` instead of `:` as separator.
